primary_complaint: Bilateral knee pain, right worse than left, x 3+ years
functional_limitations: Cannot use stairs without hand rail
Pain limits ability to demonstrate drills as volunteer coach
objective_measurements: Right knee flexion limited to 95 degrees

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (02/15/2025): Right knee: Severe tricompartmental osteoarthritis with bone-on-bone medial compartment involvement, Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV, varus deformity 8 degrees. Left knee: Moderate OA, KL Grade III, less severe than right.

surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Anterior approach. Will perform standard primary TKA.

denial_risk_flags.label: Cortisone Injection Dates Undocumented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The two corticosteroid injections are dated only as 'approx 2023, exact date unclear,' which does not meet payer documentation standards requiring specific dates for prior treatments. Aetna PPO typically requires verifiable dates for all conservative interventions.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain and document the exact dates of the cortisone injections from the administering provider's records or pharmacy records, and add a chart addendum specifying those dates.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Payer Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 6 weeks of physical therapy is documented. Aetna PPO and most major payers for CPT 27447 typically require a minimum of 6–8 weeks of formal PT with documented failure; 6 weeks is at the lower boundary and may be challenged if no functional outcome measures from PT are included.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain PT discharge summary documenting specific functional outcomes, visit count, and clinical rationale for discontinuation to substantiate failure of conservative care.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: PT at Lakeside Rehab for 6 weeks

denial_risk_flags.label: No MRI Documented for Surgical Request
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No MRI of the right knee is documented. While X-ray with KL Grade IV findings is strong evidence, some Aetna PPO policies for CPT 27447 require or prefer MRI to rule out other pathology and confirm surgical candidacy.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Consider obtaining an MRI of the right knee or document clinical rationale for why X-ray alone is sufficient given the severity of bone-on-bone KL Grade IV findings.

denial_risk_flags.label: Hyaluronic Acid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The Synvisc-One injection dated 06/2024 has no documented follow-up period or duration of observation before declaring failure. Payers may require a minimum observation window (typically 3–6 months) post-injection before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart note documenting the follow-up assessment date after the 06/2024 Synvisc-One injection and the specific clinical findings that confirmed treatment failure.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: Synvisc-One hyaluronic acid injection right knee 06/2024

denial_risk_flags.label: Elevated BMI May Require Additional Documentation
denial_risk_flags.explanation: BMI of 34.2 combined with ASA III status (HTN, DM, OSA) may trigger payer requests for pre-operative medical optimization documentation, particularly glycemic control evidence beyond A1c 7.1.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Include documentation of pre-operative medical clearance from the patient's primary care physician or endocrinologist confirming the patient is optimized for surgery.

denial_risk_flags.label: Requesting Provider Discrepancy
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart lists Dr. Sandra Reyes MD as the operating surgeon in the OR field, while Dr. Sandra Reyes is listed as the requesting provider. Payer systems may flag a mismatch between the documented operating surgeon and the provider submitting the prior authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Clarify and reconcile the operating surgeon name on the prior authorization submission to match the chart documentation, or add an addendum confirming Dr. Sandra Reyes as the attending surgeon of record.

diagnosis_codes.note: Two valid ICD-10 codes (M17.11, M17.12) are documented and directly support the requested procedure.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Four distinct conservative treatments are named: physical therapy, corticosteroid injections, hyaluronic acid injection, and OTC analgesics/NSAIDs.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Physical therapy has a documented duration (6 weeks), but corticosteroid injections and Synvisc-One injection lack documented duration or observation periods, weakening the conservative care timeline.

imaging_findings.note: X-ray with KL Grade IV findings and bone-on-bone involvement is documented with a date, providing strong radiographic support for surgical necessity.

functional_limitations.note: Three specific functional limitations are documented: walking distance under 1 block, stair use requiring handrail, and inability to perform coaching duties.

surgical_approach.note: Anterior approach for standard primary TKA is explicitly documented in the plan.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27447 is explicitly listed and matches the documented procedure of total knee arthroplasty.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration of 3+ years is explicitly documented in the HPI.
